title: Swarming contracts — attested worktree identity checks
action: Per D1/D3 and review repairs, execute in the SHARED checkout only after wt-2 caps; no parallel shared-index workers exist. Define normal opt-in worktree dispatch for enabled Claude Code waves of at least two and wt-4 as the sole validation-only one-worker exception. Before dispatch, the orchestrator must capture a control-plane attestation not sourced from worker output: canonical commonDir, canonical worktree path and native id, initial symbolic HEAD ref, base commit, and declared/reserved path set. Worktree mode is ineligible if the runtime cannot capture or retain this attestation. State the threat model: same-UID workers are cooperative/fallible, not security principals; Git metadata is consistency evidence, never independent authorization. At integration revalidate the attested path/id/commonDir/ref, require result commit descend from base, and require base..commit diff paths to be a subset of attested reservations. Worker id/branch text is informational. Detached HEAD, attestation/backlink/ref mismatch, non-descendant commit, or out-of-scope diff halts as typed identity failure. Add focused test_lib assertions for serialized eligibility, attestation fields, threat-model wording, ancestry/path-set checks, and mismatch halts. Detailed transactional disposition remains wt-4.

truths: wt-3 depends on wt-2 and no two implementation cells commit concurrently in shared checkout
normal isolation requires enabled Claude Code waves >=2; wt-4 is the sole validation exception
orchestrator captures commonDir, path/id, symbolic ref, base commit, and reserved paths before worker output
worktree mode is refused when protected control-plane attestation is unavailable
same-UID worker is not treated as a security boundary and Git metadata is consistency only
integration requires attestation match, base ancestry, and diff subset of reserved paths
focused tests prove eligibility, attestation, ancestry/path-scope, and typed mismatch halts
artifacts: serialized dispatch, threat model, attestation, and integration identity contract in bee-swarming/SKILL.md
worker result fields and non-authoritative wording in worker-details.md
prohibitions: trusting worker-reported id, branch, or base
claiming worktree isolation is a security sandbox

trace.outcome: Defined serialized opt-in native worktree dispatch with protected pre-dispatch identity attestation, consistency-only threat model, typed identity/ancestry/reserved-diff halts, and non-authoritative worker result fields; focused contract assertions pass against D1/D3 and the spike constraints recorded in docs/history/worktree-isolation/CONTEXT.md.
trace.verify_output: PASS  worktree dispatch contract: eligibility, protected attestation, consistency-only identity, and typed integration halts are explicit\n331 passed, 0 failed
